paper.title: Dataset Color Quantization: A Training-Oriented Framework for Dataset-Level Compression
paper.abstract: Large-scale image datasets are fundamental to deep learning, but their high storage demands pose challenges for deployment in resource-constrained environments. While existing approaches reduce dataset size by discarding samples, they often ignore the significant redundancy within each image -- particularly in the color space. To address this, we propose Dataset Color Quantization (DCQ), a unified framework that compresses visual datasets by reducing color-space redundancy while preserving infor

memory_insight.compression_methods: Color quantization with consistent palette representations and selective retention of semantically important colors
memory_insight.before_after_comparison: Dataset size reduction from 100GB to 20GB (80% reduction)

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing color quantization with consistent palette representations can reduce dataset size by up to 80% without compromising model training performance.
technical_details: Color quantization with consistent palette representations
Selective retention of semantically important colors
Structural details necessary for effective feature learning
Optimized memory allocation and bandwidth management
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop platforms due to its efficient memory usage and optimized compression methods.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
